# VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0007 - QUIC RFC 9001 Known Stateless Reset Loop Suppression Verification

## Scope

Verify the retained-route known-token loop-suppression matrix cell after AEAD-limit terminal discard, including suppression when the trigger is reset-shaped and carries the retained token, continued emission for different trailing tokens, exclusion of non-reset-shaped inputs, and connected endpoint-host plus listener-host no-response behavior.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0008

## Verification Method

Focused requirement-home execution, direct endpoint and endpoint-host suppression tests, deterministic fuzz coverage for retained-route token matching, BenchmarkDotNet Dry coverage for the stateless-reset helper path, and SpecTrace render/check.

## Preconditions

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0006 remains the retained-route response floor baseline.
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0007 remains the malformed-formatting suppression baseline.
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0008 defines the known-token loop-suppression cell being verified.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run focused requirement-home tests for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0008.
- Run the retained-route response and formatting matrix guards for REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0006 and REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6P6-0007.
- Run `dotnet run -c Release --project benchmarks/Incursa.Quic.Benchmarks.csproj -- --job Dry --filter "*QuicStatelessResetBenchmarks*" --artifacts artifacts/verification/20260425-rfc9001-s6p6-token-loop-suppression/BenchmarkDotNet.Artifacts` to refresh the permanent stateless-reset benchmark evidence.
- Render the touched SpecTrace Markdown views from JSON and run the render check for those scopes.
- Run `git diff --check` before committing the slice.

## Expected Result

The endpoint suppresses stateless-reset response emission before rate-budget reservation when a retained-route trigger is reset-shaped and carries the retained token for the same remote address and port. Different trailing tokens remain eligible for the retained-route response floor, non-reset-shaped inputs do not take the known-reset suppression branch, host receive-loop behavior produces no UDP response for known reset triggers, and the permanent benchmark suite covers the suppression hot path without claiming broader non-retained behavior.

Passed locally on 2026-04-25.
